findings.title: Service restart via start() after stop() will fail due to stale aiohttp app state
findings.suggested_fix: In handle_service_start(), re-create the aiohttp Application instead of reusing the stale self.app.
findings.why_it_matters: The WebAdminHandler exposes a start service button. If a user stops and restarts the service via the web UI, the second start() call will create a new Application but the routes are only registered once.
evidence: proxy.py:128-139: stop() sets client_session=None, runner=None, site=None, app=None
proxy.py:95-123: start() creates new Application but routes must be re-registered each time
web_admin.py:958-964: handle_service_start() calls proxy_server.start() without checking current state

findings.title: Uptime calculation continues after service stops
findings.suggested_fix: Reset _start_time to None when service stops.
findings.why_it_matters: When a user stops the service via the web UI, the dashboard still shows a growing uptime value because _start_time is never reset.
evidence: web_admin.py:846-849: uptime is calculated if _start_time exists, but _start_time is never cleared on stop

findings.title: Level filtering happens after pagination causing incorrect total_pages count
findings.suggested_fix: Either filter logs before pagination, or recalculate total_pages after filtering.
findings.why_it_matters: When a user filters logs by level, pagination shows wrong page count.
evidence: web_admin.py:867: get_logs_page returns all logs with total_pages based on all lines
web_admin.py:873-874: total_pages reflects all logs, not filtered subset

findings.title: Preset apply message claims restart but does not restart
findings.suggested_fix: Either actually restart the service after applying preset, or update the UI message.
findings.why_it_matters: Intent mismatch: The UI says this will restart the service but implementation only writes config file.
evidence: web_admin.py:1094-1095: JavaScript confirm dialog says this will restart service
web_admin.py:909-911: handle_preset_apply only calls apply_preset() which writes to config file

findings.title: current_upstreams variable calculated but never used
findings.suggested_fix: Either remove the unused variable, or implement the actual preset detection logic.
findings.why_it_matters: Dead code indicates incomplete implementation. Users cannot see which preset is currently active.
evidence: web_admin.py:882: current_upstreams is computed but not used
web_admin.py:886: preset_list always appends current: False

findings.title: JSON parsing catches KeyError but request.json() does not raise KeyError
findings.suggested_fix: Remove KeyError from the except clause.
findings.why_it_matters: Misleading exception handling. KeyError will never be caught.
evidence: aiohttp request.json() raises JSONDecodeError, not KeyError

findings.title: HTTP header sanitization regex may redact partial matches
findings.suggested_fix: Make the HTTP pattern more specific by requiring word boundary.
findings.why_it_matters: The regex could match URLs containing authorization: incorrectly.
evidence: log_file.py:48: http_pattern matches Authorization: anywhere in text

findings.title: Session cleanup only runs on create_session, memory leak potential
findings.suggested_fix: Add periodic cleanup or cleanup on validate_session.
findings.why_it_matters: Memory leak potential: expired sessions accumulate until next login attempt.

residual_risks.mitigation: Consider adding a service_state enum or lock

residual_risks.risk: No CSRF protection on POST endpoints
residual_risks.mitigation: SameSite=Lax cookie helps but CSRF tokens would be more secure
Consider atomic write pattern: write to temp file, then rename

testing_gaps.description: No test for stop() followed by start()
Should test pagination behavior when level filter reduces result count
No test verifying preset application affects running configuration
No test for thread safety of session operations
